phases.name: Position Identity
phases.description: Student understands what defines the position, what the objective is, and what counts as success or failure.
phases.instructor_focus: Name the position. Show the shape. Define success and failure. No technique yet.
phases.student_objective: Recognize the position. Know where you are on the fieldboard.

phases.name: Constraint Discovery
phases.description: Students feel the problem before hearing the solution. Minimal instruction. Time pressure.
phases.instructor_focus: Set a task with constraints. Observe. Do not correct yet.
phases.student_objective: Discover the core problem through direct experience.

phases.name: Reduced-Tool Isolation
phases.description: Remove tools (grips, hands, specific movements) to force discovery of balance, pressure, hips, posture, and connection.
phases.instructor_focus: Remove one or more tools. Student must solve the problem with fewer resources.
phases.student_objective: Discover structural principles before technique memorization.

phases.name: Tool Addition
phases.description: Introduce one structural tool at a time: a grip, a frame, an underhook, a control layer. Only one major addition per cycle.
phases.instructor_focus: Add one tool. Show how it changes the position. Let students repeat until the tool feels natural.
phases.student_objective: Integrate one new tool into the position without losing structural control.

phases.name: Progressive Resistance
phases.description: Increase realism gradually. Cooperative → timing → reactive → full resistance. Never jump from dead drilling to full sparring.
phases.instructor_focus: Control the resistance level. Escalate only when the student can handle the current level.
phases.student_objective: Execute the technique against increasing resistance without losing structure.

phases.name: Specific Sparring
phases.description: Start from a specific fieldboard cell or road checkpoint. Win condition is defined. Reset on completion or failure.
phases.instructor_focus: Define the start position, the win condition, and the reset rule. Keep rounds short.
phases.student_objective: Execute under realistic conditions with a clear objective.

phases.name: Live Integration
phases.description: Broader rolling with the road objective as a background goal. Student seeks the position organically.
phases.instructor_focus: Let students roll freely. Observe whether the road appears naturally.
phases.student_objective: Find and execute the road within open rolling.

phases.name: Dual Perspective
phases.description: Teach the opposite side of the same road. Survival, escape, defense, posture recovery, anti-sweep. Same road, other player.
phases.instructor_focus: Switch roles. The attacker becomes the defender. Revisit each phase briefly from the other side.
phases.student_objective: Understand both sides of the road. Recognize the attacks you will face.

resistance_levels.name: Cooperative
resistance_levels.description: Partner moves slowly and predictably. No resistance.

resistance_levels.name: Timing
resistance_levels.description: Partner moves at realistic speed but does not counter.

resistance_levels.name: Reactive
resistance_levels.description: Partner defends naturally but does not initiate attacks.

resistance_levels.name: Full
resistance_levels.description: Partner resists fully and counters. Realistic conditions.

class_structure.note: Phases 1-3 (CTRL) run first. Phases 4-5 (TRZ/SWP) follow. Phases 6-8 close the class. Not all phases appear in every class — a single road spans 2-4 class sessions.
